response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software developer with over 15 years of career history, but his background is almost entirely in web development, mobile applications, and developer tooling — none of which maps to the core requirements of this Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator optimization, and GPU or SIMD/MIMD architecture analysis. Bogdan has no demonstrated experience in any of these areas. His most hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms at Altom, low-latency microservices at Dunnhumby) is peripheral at best, and his recent AI exposure is limited to high-level agent tooling and MCP frameworks rather than deep-learning workload performance tuning at the silicon level.
strengths: Long track record of performance-conscious engineering, including low-latency service development handling millions of requests per second
Broad polyglot background (C/C++, DLang, TypeScript, Java) showing systems-level awareness beyond typical web developers
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and agentic workflows, indicating current engagement with the AI domain
concerns: Zero experience with SoC or IP modeling, which is a core and non-negotiable requirement of this role
No background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD systems, or deep neural network training and inference optimization
Career trajectory is firmly rooted in web, mobile, and developer tooling — no hardware or chip-level performance engineering at any point
